themes.description: Users feel trapped in a loop of familiar music as the algorithm reinforces existing preferences, making their musical world progressively smaller.
representative_quotes: The more I use Spotify, the more it shows me the same artists. It's like my musical world is shrinking.
Discover Weekly used to introduce me to new artists. Now it's just songs I've heard 100 times.
I want to break out of my bubble but Spotify just keeps feeding me what I already know.

themes.description: Recommendations don't account for mood, activity, time of day, or social context — the algorithm treats all listening as identical regardless of situation.
representative_quotes: I'm at a party and Spotify plays my introspective indie playlist. No context awareness at all.
Working out and it shuffles into slow acoustic songs. The algorithm has no idea what I need.
Sometimes I want upbeat, sometimes I want chill. Spotify literally can't tell the difference.

themes.description: Users have stopped using Discover Weekly, Radio, and Release Radar because they've become predictable, recycling familiar artists with minimal genuine novelty.
representative_quotes: I stopped checking Discover Weekly months ago. It's always the same type of music.
Spotify Radio is just 20 songs on repeat. How is that a discovery tool?
Release Radar shows me releases from artists I already follow. That's not discovery, that's just news.

themes.description: Heavy listening in one genre causes the algorithm to ignore all other genres the user is curious about — casual exploration permanently contaminates the main recommendation profile.
representative_quotes: I got into lo-fi for studying and now that's ALL it recommends, even when I'm not studying.
I listened to one Taylor Swift album and now it thinks I'm a pop fan. I'm not.
Can't casually explore jazz without it thinking I've permanently abandoned rock.

themes.description: Users want to discover music across languages, regions, and cultural contexts they're curious about — but the algorithm has no mechanism for cultural intent signals.
representative_quotes: I want to discover Korean indie music but it only pushes mainstream K-pop at me.
There's incredible African music I'd love to find. Spotify has no idea this matters to me.
I lived in Brazil for a year and want to explore that music. The algorithm can't process this context.

frustrations: Discover Weekly quality has declined over time — feels recycled and predictable
No way to tell Spotify 'I want something completely different from my usual'
Old liked songs bias recommendations even for tracks saved years ago
Playlist shuffle feels non-random — same songs repeatedly surface
No 'serendipity mode' or mechanism to force genuine novelty
behaviors: Manually searching artist names on YouTube to discover music outside Spotify
Asking Reddit communities for recommendations instead of using Spotify features
Creating separate 'exploration' accounts to avoid contaminating main algorithm
Using multiple streaming services simultaneously (Spotify + Apple Music + YouTube)
Saving songs to playlists defensively to 'preserve' current algorithm state
unmet_needs: A 'blind spot detector' — show me what genres, moods, and eras I've never explored
Context-aware discovery: 'I'm driving to a new city, what local artists should I know?'
Intentional novelty slider: 'I want something 70% familiar, 30% genuinely new today'
Collaborative discovery: 'Recommend music based on what me and my friends collectively love'
Taste journey mapping: 'Show me how my music taste has evolved over the past 5 years'

segments.name: The Comfort Listeners
segments.description: Play the same 5 playlists on repeat. Not actively seeking discovery but would welcome gentle nudges toward new music.
segments.pain: Algorithm has given up showing them anything new — rewards their inertia

segments.name: The Active Explorers
segments.description: Actively want to discover but find Spotify's tools insufficient. Use Reddit, YouTube, and friends as primary discovery channels.
segments.pain: High intent to discover, no reliable mechanism within Spotify to act on it

segments.name: The Contextual Listeners
segments.description: Music is deeply tied to activity and mood. Want radically different recommendations for gym vs. studying vs. cooking.
segments.pain: Algorithm ignores context entirely — treats all listening as the same signal

segments.name: The Cultural Seekers
segments.description: Want to discover music across languages, regions, and cultures. Curious about music they've been exposed to through travel, films, or social media.
segments.pain: Algorithm can't process cultural curiosity signals at all
